hypothesis: Combining STOCH_RSI mean-reversion with VORTIC trend confirmation and ATR volatility filtering creates a robust entry signal that avoids false breakouts during low-volatility periods. The VORTIC oscillator helps filter for genuine trend reversals, while ATR ensures proper risk management and position sizing.

Combining Stochastic RSI for mean-reversion signals with Vortex indicator to confirm trend strength and direction, while using ATR for dynamic volatility-based risk management, improves signal quality by filtering out weak trends and reducing whipsaw in ranging markets.

Combining STOCH_RSI mean-reversion with VORTIC volatility confirmation creates a robust entry filter that avoids false signals during low-volatility periods. The ATR-based risk management ensures proper position sizing and prevents large drawdowns during volatile market moves.

Combining STOCH_RSI with VORTIC creates a robust mean-reversion signal by identifying overextended conditions (STOCH_RSI below 20) confirmed by increasing volatility (VORTIC negative over 2 periods) and trend strength (ATR rising), reducing false signals during choppy or trending markets
